fresh_facts.claim: Operation Epic Fury has struck over 5,500 targets inside Iran, including more than 60 ships and the complete elimination of Soleimani-class warships, according to Admiral Brad Cooper's briefing on March 11, 2026.
The USS George H.W. Bush aircraft carrier completed preparations to deploy to the Eastern Mediterranean for strikes on Iran as of March 7, 2026.
The Vermont Air National Guard's 158th Fighter Wing (F-35 operators) deployed as part of ongoing operations as of March 3, 2026.
Large-scale U.S. air and naval forces remain positioned in the region, including 2 carriers and 16 surface warships, representing the largest deployment since 2003.
U.S. strikes destroyed Iranian ballistic missile manufacturing facilities in the past 24 hours as of March 11, 2026, with a facility struck 'just last night.'
Battle damage was inflicted on Chabahar port during Operation Epic Fury, with destruction of Jamaran/Bayandar-class naval vessels and warships in the Strait of Hormuz.
Iranian drone and missile attacks have dropped drastically since the start of Operation Epic Fury.

Mojtaba Khamenei was officially appointed Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts on March 8, 2026, succeeding his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei who was killed in a U.S.-Israel strike on February 28.
An elite 'black-clad' NOPO counterterrorism unit was deployed to protect newly appointed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei on March 8, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first written message calling for continued military resistance on March 12, 2026.

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian outlined three non-negotiable 'red lines' for ending the war: formal recognition of Iran's sovereign rights, financial reparations for damages, and international security guarantees against future attacks.
Iranian President Pezeshkian consulted with Russian President Vladimir Putin regarding peace conditions, signaling a shift from military retaliation to political demands.
EU envoys approved sanctions on 19 Iranian officials and entities for human rights violations on March 11, 2026 at 09:00 AM CET.
EU foreign policy chief Kallas stated that new sanctions protect EU interests amid the war and ongoing Iranian domestic repression.
Hardline IRGC elements dominate decision-making in the Iranian regime post-Khamenei death with little evidence of internal fractures despite U.S. hopes.
The Iranian regime has expanded securitization efforts and shows paranoia regarding internal stability as of March 10, 2026.
U.S. intelligence assesses a low likelihood of Iranian regime collapse even from large-scale attacks as of March 11, 2026.
Recent large-scale protests in Iran were violently suppressed, leading to fragile coercion-based stability and political limbo as of March 11, 2026.
U.S. strikes on Iranian detention facilities caused jailbreaks, including at Mariwan facility, prompting IRGC-ordered evacuations on March 3, 2026.
U.S. strikes targeted Kurdish opposition areas in Iran, with the IRGC responding by striking Kurdish opposition groups in Iraq to counter potential destabilization.
An external rally in Gothenburg on March 7, 2026 supported the NCRI's provisional government plan post-Khamenei.
ACLED analysis notes potential for chaos from U.S. strikes on peripheral areas, which could stretch Iranian security forces.
Intensified airstrikes as of March 11, 2026 have targeted Iranian infrastructure, causing market swings and oil backstop mobilization amid Strait of Hormuz attacks.
The ongoing U.S.-Israel military campaign has been ongoing for 12 days as of March 11, 2026.
The NCRI stated that regime overthrow will not come via foreign war alone, reflecting opposition views on political transition.
Analysts warn that regime change through military means risks prolonged instability over stability in Iran.

regime_fall.current_status: On day 12 of the US-Israeli campaign, Operation Epic Fury has struck over 5,500 targets including naval assets and ballistic missile facilities, while Mojtaba Khamenei consolidates succession control despite escalating internal security challenges, drone strikes targeting Tehran security infrastructure, and mobilization failures amid critical supply shortages.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first written message on March 12, 2026 calling for continued military resistance while simultaneously facing cascading military losses from Operation Epic Fury (5,500+ targets struck including naval fleet destruction and missile manufacturing facilities), signaling regime defiance amid structural collapse.
